mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and relaxes financial conditions, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is trending down on a 1- to 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in most periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that weakens earnings expectations and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month once the unemployment rate has broken higher by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST is also rising, retail demand and housing activity jointly signal broad private-sector strength, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone, or if its 20-day forward return edge disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent drop in INDPRO indicates industrial output weakness and inventory overhang, which compresses cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after 2-month declines in INDPRO, or if the signal's sign flips to positive in multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in GDPC1 confirms demand contraction that should lower corporate revenue growth expectations and pressure equity prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls on a quarterly annualized basis for 1 quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 prints are not followed by below-zero 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates stay deeply negative and investors price future policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, low-rate episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value beyond CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll contraction and weaker household income, which should depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if 1-week forward SPX returns are not negative after payroll declines, or if PAYEMS changes fail to beat a random benchmark in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST combined with rising RSAFS reflects improving housing turnover and consumer spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month; the signal fails when housing activity improves without retail sales confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive HOUST changes without concurrent RSAFS improvement do not underperform the joint-upturn condition, or if the joint signal's hit rate falls below 55% over any rolling 3-year window.
